top_signals.title: Treasuries’ Oil-Driven Selloff Stalls as Inflation Gauge Slows
top_signals.summary: Treasuries rallied back to be little-changed on the day, erasing earlier declines spurred by higher oil prices, after a key US inflation gauge rose less than expected.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: US munitions depleted by Iran war will take years to restore, analysis finds
top_signals.summary: Replenishing stockpiles of critical weapons systems could take until 2030 or 2031, creating a "window of vulnerability," a new analysis found.

top_signals.title: Russia Selling Yuan Bonds After Putin’s Visit to Beijing
top_signals.summary: Russia is selling its second issue of yuan-denominated sovereign bonds after President Vladimir Putin held talks in Beijing last week.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Fed’s Williams Says Rates Impact of Productivity Boom Is Unclear
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams says the answer to whether a lift in productivity will ultimately impact interest rates and the central bank’s policymaking is, “unsurprisingly, it depends.” Williams spoke Thursday at a central banking conference in Iceland. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Lloyd’s Register Announces Nigerian Future Navigators Schools Program Pilot
top_signals.summary: Lloyd’s Register (LR) is launching its Future Navigators Schools Programme pilot in Lagos, Nigeria, aimed at inspiring young people to explore maritime and STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics) careers through in-person interactive workshops and a bespoke mobile game.
